message:
fix(fj-cache): harden golden cache key verifier against truncated/renamed snapshots (frankenjax-a7xi)

verify_golden_keys zipped the supplied golden vec with the
current capture_golden_keys() result and only checked the digest
field on each pair. Two silent failure modes:

1. If the golden snapshot was truncated (fewer entries than the
   current ref set), the zip stopped at golden.len(), so any
   newly-added cache key reference passed verification without
   ever being checked. A regression that added a new
   cache-key-affecting code path AND added a golden entry that
   didn't actually exercise it would slip through.

2. If golden entries were reordered or renamed, the zip would
   pair the descriptors mismatchedly and emit "DRIFT: A:
   expected=hash_of_A, actual=hash_of_B" — confusing the next
   reader who would chase a digest delta when the actual problem
   was a misordered golden.

Add four explicit checks ahead of the digest comparison:

- GOLDEN_REF_COUNT_MISMATCH: report when golden.len() !=
  current.len() so a truncated snapshot fails immediately.
- GOLDEN_REF_DESCRIPTION_MISMATCH[i]: report when desc !=
  current_desc at the same index. The DRIFT message that follows
  uses the current_desc so the reader sees what the impl actually
  produced, not what the snapshot claimed.
- MISSING_GOLDEN_REF[i]: when the current capture has more
  entries than the snapshot, list the entries that were never
  checked. This catches the "added a cache key but forgot to
  freeze it" case directly.
- EXTRA_GOLDEN_REF[i]: when the snapshot has more entries than
  the current capture, list the entries that were dropped from
  the impl. This catches the "removed a cache key but forgot to
  re-bless the snapshot" case directly.

DRIFT[i] now includes the index for easier triage when multiple
entries drift in the same run.

Beads frankenjax-a7xi (Harden cache golden verifier against
truncated snapshots, priority 2, type bug, status in_progress)
captures the diagnosis and the planned regression tests
(truncated and renamed golden snapshots must fail). The added
checks here implement the fix; the regression tests covering the
new error variants land in the next slice.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
